name: 100 Grit
description: Touch-controlled transistor-ladder LPF, OTA VCA, and vintage-headphone-amp distortion with 8 brass touchpoints. Normalized feedback paths, x100 gain switch, and a secondary unstable distortion comparator turn it into a self-contained performance noise box.

controls.name: IN 1
controls.description: Input attenuator for IN 1 jack. Also sends DIST 2 comparator into filter when unpatched, creating extreme square-wave chaos as a noise source.

controls.name: IN 2
controls.description: Input attenuator for IN 2 jack. Normals DIST output back into the mixer as an opposing resonance path when unpatched.

controls.name: FREQUENCY
controls.description: Cutoff of the transistor-ladder 4-pole LPF. Large centre knob. Must be up to pass audio.

controls.name: FM 1
controls.description: Attenuator for FM 1 CV input. When unpatched, normals DIST output into filter cutoff for dynamic audio-rate FM — laser/zap percussion.

controls.name: FM 2
controls.description: Attenuator for FM 2 CV input. Used for V/Oct tracking when patched.

controls.name: RES
controls.description: Filter resonance bias. Self-oscillates when pushed; threshold set by internal RV3 trim.

controls.name: RES CV
controls.description: Attenuator for RES CV input. Unpatched, normals DIST output as audio-rate resonance modulation for screeches riding on the resonant peak.

controls.name: GAIN
controls.description: VCA gain. Also drives the distortion circuit harder. No sound without some initial gain.
controls.detail: Overdrives above ~75% · gates both OUT and DIST

controls.name: GAIN CV
controls.description: Attenuator for GAIN CV input. Unpatched, normals filter POLE 2 (12dB output) into VCA CV for subtle harmonic-shift distortion.

controls.name: x100 Switch
controls.description: Three-position gain multiplier. OFF = normal. ON = x100 always. (ON) = momentary spring x100 for extreme distortion bursts.

inputs.name: IN 1
inputs.description: Primary filter input. Mixed with IN 2 into the ladder filter.
inputs.normalizedTo: DIST 2 comparator output (square-wave chaos noise source)

inputs.name: FM 1
inputs.normalizedTo: DIST output (dynamic FM from distortion signal)

inputs.name: FM 2
inputs.description: Filter-frequency CV input. Used for V/Oct tracking.

inputs.name: GAIN CV
inputs.description: VCA CV input. 5V = unity, more = gain above unity.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: VCA output following the filter. Jumper J10 can re-route this to direct filter output (pre-VCA).

outputs.name: DIST
outputs.description: Distortion-amp output after the VCA. Vintage headphone-amp circuit driven into instability. Self-limiting.

behaviors.name: 8 brass touchpoints
behaviors.trigger: finger, cable tip, alligator clip on touchpoint
behaviors.description: Eight brass balls expose internal nodes as resistive patch points: DIST 2 OUT, GAIN CV, DIST 2 AMP PIN, RES CV, DIST 1 AMP PIN, FREQ CV, DIST OUT, GAIN CV. Inputs are adjacent to outputs so any two-point touch creates a connection. Bridge with fingers, cables, or conductive material for performance distortion.

behaviors.name: Feedback normalization
behaviors.trigger: leave input unpatched · turn up its attenuator
behaviors.description: Each audio/CV input normalizes to a hidden internal feedback path. IN 1→DIST 2, IN 2→DIST, FM 1→DIST, RES CV→DIST, GAIN CV→POLE 2. Turning an input's attenuator becomes a feedback amount control — the module self-generates sound without any input cable.

behaviors.name: x100 momentary
behaviors.description: Momentary side of the gain switch springs back to OFF when released. Lets players punch in extreme distortion bursts without latching — transient-shaped chaos controlled by the switch.

behaviors.name: DIST 2 secret comparator
behaviors.trigger: IN 1 attenuator turned up with no cable
behaviors.description: DIST 2 is an unstable distortion amp that compares the VCA output with the DIST output. Not available at any jack — only reachable via normalization or the DIST 2 OUT touchpoint. Produces extreme square-wave chaos ideal for noise-box patches.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillating sine source
behaviors.trigger: RES CW · no input · FREQUENCY swept
behaviors.description: Ladder filter self-oscillates into a clean sine. Plays V/Oct via FM 2 as a filter-voice oscillator. Calibration trim RV3 sets the self-oscillation threshold.

behaviors.name: J9 Input-to-Resonance jumper
behaviors.description: Default ON: preserves bass and prevents resonance volume loss (classic-synth style). Removed: resonance takes over the input, whistling character, self-oscillation overtakes the signal.

behaviors.name: J10 Output Source jumper
behaviors.description: Default VCA: OUT is VCA-affected. Switched: OUT taken directly from filter output, bypassing the VCA so GAIN only controls DIST. Turns 100 Grit into two independent filter+distortion outs.

behaviors.name: Noise-box mode
behaviors.trigger: no inputs · all attenuators ~25–60% · x100 ON · play touchpoints
behaviors.description: With nothing patched, the normalizations form a self-oscillating feedback network. Playing two or more touchpoints simultaneously modulates the internal signal paths — screams, radio-pickup artifacts, and rhythmic squealing chaos.

patchIdeas.name: Touchpoint performance distortion
patchIdeas.patch: Drum loop → IN 1 @ 75% · FREQUENCY mid · GAIN 75% · x100 OFF · play the 4 right touchpoints with a thumb · adjacent input/output pairs create resistive patches in real time
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* IN 1: 75%
* FREQUENCY: ~12 o'clock
* GAIN: 75%
* x100: OFF

Drums:

- Drums (Out) -> 100 Grit (IN 1)
- 100 Grit (DIST) -> Output (In) // play touchpoints live

patchIdeas.name: Self-feedback noise box
patchIdeas.patch: No inputs · IN 1 at 25% · IN 2 at 25% · GAIN 60% · GAIN CV 60% · RES CV 25% · x100 ON · listen to DIST · play all 8 touchpoints for screeching chaos
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* IN 1: 25%
* IN 2: 25%
* GAIN: 60%
* GAIN CV: 60%
* RES CV: 25%
* x100: ON

- 100 Grit (DIST) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Laser percussion FM
patchIdeas.patch: Kick trigger → envelope → FM 2 @ high attenuation · FM 1 unpatched (DIST normalled in for dynamic FM) · RES mid · GAIN 75% · filter pings into screaming laser tails
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* RES: ~2 o'clock
* GAIN: 75%
* FM 1: up (DIST feedback normalled)

Envelope:

Trigger:

- Trigger (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> 100 Grit (FM 2)
- 100 Grit (DIST) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating sine voice
patchIdeas.patch: No input · RES full CW · V/Oct → FM 2 · GAIN CV from ADSR · 100 Grit sings as a ladder-sine voice with distortion character via x100
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* RES: full CW
* GAIN: 0
* x100: OFF

Sequencer:

ADSR:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> 100 Grit (FM 2)
- ADSR (Out) >> 100 Grit (GAIN CV)
- 100 Grit (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dynamic audio-rate FM resonance
patchIdeas.patch: Synth pad → IN 1 · RES mid · RES CV up with unpatched RES CV (DIST normalled for audio-rate res mod) · screeching harmonics ride the pad's resonance
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* IN 1: 50%
* RES: ~1 o'clock
* RES CV: 60% (DIST normalled)
* GAIN: 60%

Pad:

- Pad (Out) -> 100 Grit (IN 1)
- 100 Grit (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: x100 punch-in explosions
patchIdeas.patch: Bass sequence → IN 1 · normal filter settings · perform x100 (ON) momentary spring for punctuation distortion bursts during a riff · spring returns to clean
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* IN 1: 75%
* FREQUENCY: ~12 o'clock
* GAIN: 60%
* x100: momentary (ON) on accents

Seq:

- Seq (Out) -> 100 Grit (IN 1)
- 100 Grit (DIST) -> Output (In) // live x100 punches

patchIdeas.name: Cable-as-touchpoint ring mod
patchIdeas.patch: Patch cable tip from external LFO into GAIN CV touchpoint · brass ball receives the CV via resistive connection · gain modulates audio-rate for AM/ring-mod effects between inputs and touchpoints
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* IN 1: 50%
* GAIN: 60%

LFO:

Source:

- Source (Out) -> 100 Grit (IN 1)
- LFO (Out) >> 100 Grit (GAIN CV) // via brass touchpoint
- 100 Grit (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual-output splitter
patchIdeas.patch: Flip J10 jumper to direct-filter · OUT = clean filter, DIST = distorted · stereo send: clean L + dirty R · GAIN controls only the distorted side
patchIdeas.diagram:
100 Grit:
* J10 jumper: direct (filter tap)
* IN 1: 75%
* GAIN: ~2 o'clock

Source:

- Source (Out) -> 100 Grit (IN 1)
- 100 Grit (OUT) -> Output (In L) // clean filtered
- 100 Grit (DIST) -> Output (In R) // distorted
